questions.question: How long should you try using your sleep toolkit before seeking professional help after a major life change disrupts your sleep?
questions.explanation: If a significant life change disrupts your sleep and the disruption has not resolved after 2-3 weeks of consistent use of your sleep toolkit, it is recommended to reach out to a healthcare provider.

questions.question: Which symptoms should prompt immediate consultation with a healthcare provider?

options.text: Loud snoring, gasping or choking during sleep, and observed pauses in breathing
Preferring to sleep with the window open

questions.explanation: Loud snoring, gasping/choking, and observed pauses in breathing are symptoms of sleep apnea and should be discussed with a healthcare provider as soon as possible. Sleep apnea is treatable and often significantly improves both sleep quality and overall health.

questions.question: What defines chronic insomnia according to the screening checklist?

options.text: Difficulty sleeping occurring 3+ nights per week for 3+ months
Needing more than 10 minutes to fall asleep

questions.explanation: Chronic insomnia is defined as difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or getting restful sleep that occurs 3 or more nights per week and persists for 3 months or longer.

questions.question: What should you do after a late social night according to the stress test protocol?

options.text: Sleep in as late as possible to make up for lost sleep
Get up at your regular time the next morning to protect your circadian rhythm
Take a sleeping pill the next night

questions.explanation: After a late social night, get up at your regular time the next morning. You may be tired, but this protects your circadian rhythm. You will naturally feel sleepier the following evening.

questions.question: Why is seeking professional help for persistent sleep difficulties described as wisdom rather than failure?

options.text: Because sleep difficulties often have multiple contributing factors and professional guidance offers additional tools that complement self-help strategies
Because it is required by law

questions.explanation: Sleep difficulties at significant levels often have multiple contributing factors. Professional guidance can offer additional tools -- including evaluation for sleep disorders, medication management, and specialized therapy -- that complement everything learned in the course.

title: Your Journey Forward and Sleep Disorder Screening - Quiz
